nodes.label: Enterprise Domain Model 4.7
nodes.description: The Enterprise domain model (EDM) is an outside-in representation of Danske Bank Group's activities, i.e. "what" the Group is doing.

nodes.label: Relationships
nodes.description: Customer information and overview that provides the basis for advisory and sales of products and services.

nodes.label: Customer Relationship Management
nodes.description: Contact management, Tracking customer interactions, Sales management, Actionable insights, Customer segmentation, Customer activities and interactions. Periodic pro-active contact.

nodes.label: Customer Financial Overview
nodes.description: General advisory of customers that looks across product and service domains such as needs analysis, budget analysis, consent management,

nodes.label: Customer Engagements Overview
nodes.description: Management of customer agreements

nodes.label: Customer Preferences
nodes.description: Registration and storage of preferences of customers about how they want to interact with the Group and the products and services of the Group.

nodes.label: Customer Segmentation
nodes.description: Segmentation of customers

nodes.label: Sales
nodes.description: Sales of products and services to customers of the Group

nodes.label: Customer Account Planning
nodes.description: Planning of changes to the customer's account and relationship with the Group, incl. for example cross-selling.

nodes.label: Product Ordering
nodes.description: Ordering of products and orchestration of fulfilment

nodes.label: Leads Management
nodes.description: Creation of new potential business, with through next best offerings for existing customers or through a variety of marketing campaigns or programs.

nodes.label: Welcoming
nodes.description: Welcoming and on-boarding of new customers

nodes.label: Partner Relationship Management
nodes.description: Management of relations with partners of the Group. Partners can be sub-contractors (for outsourcing), businesses that provides adjacent services that can deployed in combination with products and services of the Group

nodes.label: Partner Programs
nodes.description: Management and configuration of partnership programs

nodes.label: Partner Management
nodes.description: Management of partnership relations, including their contracts.

nodes.label: End of Relationship
nodes.description: Termination of the relationship with a customer - both voluntary and involuntary termination.

nodes.label: Estate Handling
nodes.description: Handling of estates following the death of a customer

nodes.label: Offboarding
nodes.description: Exiting and off-boarding of customers

nodes.label: Customer Care
nodes.description: Engage and service the customers and potential customers to address their needs and concerns concerning the financial relationship with the Group.

nodes.label: Customer Scenario Analysis
nodes.description: Analysis of financial scenario of the customer entire financial situation across all financial engagements, products and services in defined scenarios or life events.

nodes.label: Customer Requests
nodes.description: Processing, triage and visitation of requests received from customer as well as ensuring a response of findings and outcomes to the customer is delivered.

nodes.label: Customer Meetings
nodes.description: Planning, preparation and execution of meetings with customers.

nodes.label: Customer Advisory
nodes.description: Advisory to the customer on banking related concerns and solution choices

nodes.label: Customer Complaints
nodes.description: Registration and handling of complaints filed by customers.

nodes.label: Involved Parties
nodes.description: Managing knowledge of legal entities that have or might have dealings with the bank. This includes access to the public CVR and CPR registers.

nodes.label: Roles & Mandates
nodes.description: Individuals, Businesses and Organisation that have a relation with one of the Group's entities may act in the capacity of a certain role or under a given mandate. The Employee of a corporate customer may for example be mandated to manage the accounts of that corporate customer.

nodes.label: Businesses & Organisations
nodes.description: Businesses are either third party legal entities that may have relation with one of the Group's entities or legal entities in the Group. An organisation is part of a Business. In the Group, they often have a distinct registration number.

nodes.label: Individuals
nodes.description: Physical individuals

nodes.label: Organisational Support
nodes.description: Shared function for supporting the organisation to operate.

nodes.label: IT Management
nodes.description: Management of the IT asset, application portfolio and associated investments

nodes.label: Operational Management
nodes.description: Execution and monitoring of the business of the bank including IT parts

nodes.label: Operational Insights
nodes.description: Overviews, dashboards, and insights about the operational performance of the enterprise's processes, services and activities.

nodes.label: Process Management
nodes.description: Activities related to structuring, defining, documenting, monitoring, and improving business processes.

nodes.label: Case Management
nodes.description: Managing a set of activities, which at times can be dynamic and unpredictable in both scope and sequence

nodes.label: Human Capital
nodes.description: Management of the people in the organisation including structure, remuneration, recruitment

nodes.label: Employee Payroll, Benefits & Compensation
nodes.description: Management of employee compensation, benefits policies, and payroll processes.

nodes.label: Learning
nodes.description: Management of learning and development technology and services.

nodes.label: Talent Management
nodes.description: Management of employee evaluations, retention and strategic staffing-decisions support tools.

nodes.label: Employee Data Management
nodes.description: Management of employee records, including employee reference details, summary activity, qualification, and assessment details as necessary to support analyses of the overall workforce for planning purposes for all HR related functions.

nodes.label: Recruitment
nodes.description: Management of the overall process of identifying, attracting, screening, shortlisting, and interviewing, suitable candidates for jobs (either permanent or temporary) within an organization.

nodes.label: Procurement
nodes.description: Procurement and purchase of external products including associated vendor management

nodes.label: Invoice-to-Pay
nodes.description: Management payment for invoiced products and services

nodes.label: Procure-to-Invoice
nodes.description: Management of purchase-requisition and purchase-order generation.

nodes.label: Source-to-Contract
nodes.description: Management of sourcing strategies and contracts.

nodes.label: Supplier Management
nodes.description: Management and supplier agreement and performance

nodes.label: End-User Collaboration
nodes.description: Technologies an solutions that enable productive collaboration between people, in physical presence or over the IT network

nodes.label: Voice Technology
nodes.description: Internal telephony, external voice connectivity with telcos, PSTN.

nodes.label: Employee Productivity
nodes.description: A standard set of desktop applications that enable collaboration and content generation.

nodes.label: Facility & Equipment Management
nodes.description: Management of physical facilities and physical equipment including buildings and meeting room contents.

nodes.label: Business Control
nodes.description: Control and oversight functions to ensure that business is executed within defined policies and limits.

nodes.label: Anti-Bribery, Corruption and Tax Evasion
nodes.description: Prevention and controls related to the anti-bribery, corruption, and tax evasion instruction.

nodes.label: Gifts and Hospitality
nodes.description: Management of gifts and hospitality received or provided, including rejection of such offers, in order to avoid potential bribery and corruption leading to conflicts of interest.

nodes.label: Auditing
nodes.description: Auditing and reconciliation of transacted business and other business activity. Auditing evaluates and improves the effectiveness of risk management, control, and governance processes

nodes.label: Compliance
nodes.description: Business activities conducted for the primary purpose of staying compliant, incl. controls and regulatory reporting

nodes.label: MAR
nodes.description: Management of market abuse reporting

nodes.label: MiFiD2
nodes.description: Management of MiFiD2 reporting

nodes.label: Compliance Advisory
nodes.description: Proactive or reactive, independent advisory on compliance topics.  It includes the review and challenge provided as part of product or service reviews.

nodes.label: Compliance Oversight
nodes.description: Controlling of compliance risk assessments, compliance monitoring and testing activities, conflicts of interest oversight, ongoing monitoring, and surveillance, such as 2 LoD trade surveillance and 2 LoD transaction monitoring. This domain also includes the combined or aggregated output of the above listed oversight activities, included in various 2 LoD compliance reports.

nodes.label: GDPR
nodes.description: Management of GDPR policies and reporting

nodes.label: Compliance Investigations & Whistleblowing
nodes.description: Whistleblowing and Compliance Investigations according to Group policies, including actions regarding Whistleblowing reports and concerns and mitigation of risks identified from Investigations.

nodes.label: Regulatory Interactions
nodes.description: Interactions with the regulators such as questions, coordination of answers, as set out in the Compliance Policy, for Group Compliance.

nodes.label: Treasury
nodes.description: Balancing and managing the daily cash flow and liquidity of funds within the bank. Popularly: “The bank in the bank”

nodes.label: Anti-Fraud Management
nodes.description: Prevention, identification, and response to fraudulent activity conduced in the bank

nodes.label: Investment Fraud
nodes.description: Investment fraud refers to deceptive practices in the financial market aimed at inducing potential investors to make decisions based on false information. This can include financial statement misrepresentation, where companies falsify their financial reports to appear more profitable or stable than they are, misleading shareholders and potential investors.

nodes.label: Card Fraud
nodes.description: Management of card fraud including detection, mitigation, and resolution

nodes.label: Payment Fraud
nodes.description: Management of payment fraud including detection, mitigation, and resolution.

nodes.label: Internal Fraud
nodes.description: Internal fraud is wrongful on unlawful deception, committed or supported by a party internal to the ban, with intent of direct or indirect financial gain detrimental to the Group and/or customers.

nodes.label: Lending Fraud
nodes.description: Management of lending fraud including detection, mitigation, and resolution

nodes.label: Identity Fraud
nodes.description: Management of identity fraud including detection, mitigation and resolution

nodes.label: Legal
nodes.description: Assessments, endorsements, and solicitation conduct by attorneys-at-law

nodes.label: Legal Document Management
nodes.description: Management of legal document in accordance with tight secrecy and longevity requirements.

nodes.label: Debt Management
nodes.description:
General management of the situation where debtors fail to meet their obligations (non-performing accounts).
This domain contains capabilities to keep track of missed obligations, remind customers (dunning), 
collect debt, outsource debt collection, tax reporting, process non-collection cases (e.g. bankruptcy and estate of deceased person),
calculate accrued interest after termination, etc.

The domain covers the entire journey, from early-stage collection to late and legal collection.

nodes.label: Late Collection
nodes.description: Late Collection takes in effect when the facility/claim is taken debt collection. The original terms and agreements are terminated, and the facility is now under the debt collection regulation. This domain covers both processes and system-of-records capabilities. Examples of capabilities inside the domain: Claim foundation, interest calculation, statute of limitation, agreements, foreclosure, debt collection agency outsourcing, bankruptcies, debt relief, estate of deceased persons, payment handling, bookkeeping, tax reporting and etc.

nodes.label: Predelinquency
nodes.description:
This is the domain that supports the processes around predelinquent customers. Predelinquency is before a customer becomes delinquent (i.e. the customer has missed an obligation and is in excess), but has a higher risk of becoming delinquent. A predelinqency indicator can be if the customer doesn't have any deposits on their salary accounts for over a month.
The purpose of the domain is to identify predelinquent customers and take appropriate actions to minimize the risk of the customer going into debt collection. Ultimately this leads to higher customer satisfaction and less credit losses for the bank.

nodes.label: Early Collection
nodes.description: Early collection is the process that starts when the customer misses an obligation (e.g. the customer has missed to pay an invoice) and ends when the facility is transferred to debt collection. The length of this process is regulated (in Denmark it is 90 days). During this process the bank will try to recover the facility back to a normal state. This includes both the hard dunning process (reminder letters) with fees and the soft dunning process (SMS, phone calls, etc.). It is a win for both the bank (less impairments and credit losses) and the customer if the facility is recovered back to a normal state.

nodes.label: Business Direction
nodes.description: Strategic and directional setting for the business model at a general level

nodes.label: Enterprise Architecture
nodes.description: The analysis, evaluation, design, and management of an organization's business and IT capabilities, Solutions, and their dependencies to optimize or effectively transition them to meet defined goals.

nodes.label: Strategic Direction
nodes.description: Management of strategies of the Group

nodes.label: Strategy Execution
nodes.description: Planning and execution of strategies, including project management, progress monitoring and follow-up.

nodes.label: AML KYC
nodes.description: Management of compliance and obligations within the Anti-Money Laundering laws

nodes.label: AML Monitoring
nodes.description: Monitoring and screening of customer business transactions to detect activities and behaviour that might be money laundering

nodes.label: Know Your Customer
nodes.description: Management of knowledge specific to AML/KYC about natural persons and legal entities

nodes.label: Customer & On-going Due Diligence
nodes.description: Management of customer due diligence, screening, ongoing due diligence and extended due diligence

nodes.label: AML Customer Screening
nodes.description: Screening of customers to identify undesired customers for example on governmental sanction lists and with regards to adverse media attention

nodes.label: AML Customer Risk Rating
nodes.description: Management of rating models that score the risk of a customer being a financial criminal

nodes.label: Financial Management
nodes.description: Managing the finances of the enterprise, including auditing, financial reporting

nodes.label: Tax Management
nodes.description: Calculation and reporting of tax on customer holdings and transactions as required by national law.

nodes.label: Customer Profitability
nodes.description: Management and measurement of customer profitability

nodes.label: Budgeting
nodes.description: Management of the organisations budgets

nodes.label: Financial & Regulatory Reporting
nodes.description: Management of financial reporting as defined by the law and financial supervisory agencies

nodes.label: Expense Management
nodes.description: Management and reimbursement of expenses

nodes.label: Financial Analysis & Reporting
nodes.description: Management of financial analysis and performance reporting

nodes.label: Financial Accounting
nodes.description: Management of the general ledger

nodes.label: Business Support
nodes.description: Shared function for supporting business executions.

nodes.label: Market Data
nodes.description: Providing information from outside the bank.

nodes.label: Credit Rating
nodes.description: Management of market data concerning credit ratings and credit quality

nodes.label: Machinery, Vehicles and Equipment Data
nodes.description: Reference information about machinery, equipment, and vehicles published or otherwise made available from public and commercial sources.

nodes.label: Other Market Data Types
nodes.description: Management of other types of market data

nodes.label: Financial Markets Market Data
nodes.description: Management of market data concerning financial markets

nodes.label: Property Data
nodes.description: Management of property data from land registries

nodes.label: Legal Entities and Natural Persons Reference Data
nodes.description: Management of market data concerning legal entities, such as their identity and ownership structures

nodes.label: Geographic Data
nodes.description: Reference geographical information such as postal codes, cities, address, municipalities from certified, public sources

nodes.label: Societal Engagement
nodes.description: The bank's societal engagement. This covers environmental, sustainable, and societal information and includes customer classification and due diligence processes around this.

nodes.label: Product Management
nodes.description: Lifecycle management of products, incl. development, making them sales-ready, and retiring them from the customers/market.

nodes.label: Product Catalogue
nodes.description: The product catalogue provides a central service to reference product specifications and related details. The broad range of specification information available includes: Product feature descriptions; pricing and eligibility requirements used in sales and on-boarding; servicing support and guidelines; and operational and production requirements.

nodes.label: Product Development
nodes.description: Management and execution of the processes of taking a product from conceptualization through design to its finished state.

nodes.label: Product Pricing
nodes.description: Management of product pricing rules

nodes.label: Marketing & Communications
nodes.description: Promoting and selling products and services, including market research and advertising.

nodes.label: Internal Communications
nodes.description: Management of marketing and communication directed towards employees

nodes.label: External Marketing & Communication
nodes.description: Management of marketing and communication directed towards external parties

nodes.label: Enterprise Content Management
nodes.description: Document generation, scanning, archiving, printing

nodes.label: Customer Documents
nodes.description: Management and distribution of documents and statements for customers

nodes.label: Digital Signing
nodes.description: Management of digital signing

nodes.label: Internal Enterprise Content
nodes.description: Management of content that is made available to management and employees

nodes.label: External Content
nodes.description: Management of content that is made available to external parties

nodes.label: Business Insight
nodes.description: Collecting and managing data about business activities. Performing research, reporting and analysis.

nodes.label: Risk Management
nodes.description: The identification, analysis, assessment, control, and avoidance, minimization, or elimination of unacceptable risks.

nodes.label: IT and Security risk
nodes.description: Technology risk management is the practices of mitigating losses from failure in technology

nodes.label: Data risk
nodes.description: Risk of loss due to untimely or flawed decision making based on insufficient or inappropriate data. Insufficient or inappropriate data can occur due to misapplication of data, unfit Data Architecture, or poor data quality

nodes.label: Credit Risk
nodes.description: Credit risk management is the practice of mitigating losses by understanding the adequacy of a bank's capital and loan loss reserves at any given time

nodes.label: Credit Budgeting
nodes.description: Creation of budgets for the customer's financials

nodes.label: Credit Exposure
nodes.description: Credit Exposure is an credit overview system that is calculating what Danske Bank is risking on a counter-party: Customer, Family, Group of companies, Companies incl. other banks and financial institutions. It gives a complete overview of all facilities

nodes.label: Collateral Management
nodes.description: Management of collateral agreements underwritten to reduce credit risk

nodes.label: Impairment
nodes.description: Management of impaired debt obligations

nodes.label: Credit Application Processing
nodes.description: Management of the process of evaluating, deciding, and granting a credit

nodes.label: Credit Underwriting
nodes.description: Decision making whether to grant loans and credits as well as management of lending authority.

nodes.label: Liquidity risk
nodes.description: Liquidity, Funding & Capital Risk risk management is the practice of mitigating losses by ensuring sufficient availability of capital and positive net cash flow.

nodes.label: Market Risk
nodes.description: Market risk management is the practice of mitigating losses by maintaining the variability of the bank's balance due movements in the financial markets within defined bands as defined by a market risk policy.

nodes.label: Life insurance & pension risk
nodes.description: Insurance and pension risk management is the practice of mitigating losses by understanding the adequacy of the funds requires to meet insurance and pension obligations

nodes.label: Operational risk
nodes.description: Operational risk management is the practice of mitigating losses from operational incidents

nodes.label: Model risk
nodes.description: Model risk management is the practice of understanding the uncertainty of key models used for measuring risk.

nodes.label: Regulatory compliance risk
nodes.description: Conduction risk is the practice of mitigating losses from failures in how business is conducted

nodes.label: Strategic risk
nodes.description: Financial control and strategic risk management is the practice of mitigate losses from failures in strategic direction setting, strategy execution as well as failures to apply defined accounting standards and comply with tax reporting

nodes.label: Financial crime risk
nodes.description: Financial crime risk management is the practice of mitigating losses from financial crime

nodes.label: Products & Services
nodes.description: Product and services offered to customers, partners, and ecosystem players. This includes full lifecycle and agreements of products and services.

nodes.label: Corporate Finance
nodes.description: Resolution of funding and capital structure of corporations, including capital budgeting & sourcing and working capital management. Capital budgeting & sourcing includes activities such as investment funding, Initial Public Offering (IPO), Merger & Acquisition (M&A), Reverse Takeover (RTO). Working capital management includes activities such as asset and liability management, operating capital management and funding

nodes.label: Financial Markets
nodes.description: Trading, hedging clearing, settlement and management of financial securities and derivatives.

nodes.label: Trade Execution
nodes.description: Management of settlements and clearing of trades

nodes.label: Trading
nodes.description: Trading and hedging of financial securities and derivatives.

nodes.label: Trading Support
nodes.description: Management of information sources and trading books

nodes.label: Asset Custody
nodes.description: Management of custodian accounts

nodes.label: Corporate Actions
nodes.description: Management of corporate actions on listed securities

nodes.label: Trading Infrastructure
nodes.description: Management of information and technology specialised for trading.

nodes.label: Investment Services
nodes.description: Provide investment portfolio and trading services for customers.

nodes.label: Investment Portfolio Planning
nodes.description: Agree the customer investment portfolio governing principles, risk appetite, management/trading guidelines and target portfolio profile. Ensure disclosures and related eligibility, suitability and other regulatory obligations are addressed and reflected in the agreement

nodes.label: Investment Portfolio Analyses
nodes.description: Perform scheduled and ad-hoc performance analysis, comparison and reporting on a customer's investment portfolio. Ability to track prices and research relating to customer’s investment portfolio.

nodes.label: Investment Portfolio Management
nodes.description: Orchestrate the investment / rebalancing of an investment portfolio to optimize gains remaining within the terms of the portfolio 'charter' or agreement. Enable customers to trade securities on their own account through the bank.

nodes.label: Financing
nodes.description:
Delivers financing solutions to customers to provide funding for something they couldn't otherwise afford.
The domain embraces a broad spectrum of responsibilities, including the development and management of financing products such as loans, mortgages, credit cards, lines of credit, factoring and leasing. It oversees the entire product lifecycle from inception, development, launch, management, to eventual decommissioning.
Beyond product development and management, the "Financing Products" domain also contains the processes involved in selling these products to customers. This includes the development and enhancement of self-service capabilities for customers to explore, compare, and purchase products independently, as well as supporting advisory capabilities for personalized service and guidance to customers.

nodes.label: Supply Chain Finance
nodes.description: Management of financing of the cost along the supply chain

nodes.label: Realkredit Loans
nodes.description: Management of mortgage loans based on the DK realkredit law with real estate collateral and match funding. Includes product capabilities, lifecycle processes for mortgage loans, and other capabilities specific to management of realkredit loans.

nodes.label: Trade Finance
nodes.description: Financing a trade of physical assets, where the exchange of funds and goods is displaced in time and location, e.g., house, overseas shipments

nodes.label: Business Financing
nodes.description: Financing services, case management, and sales processes specifically targeted Business Customers for other purposes than property financing. Business financing cases include products from other product domains, primarily Bank Loans & Credit Facilities, Cards and Accounts.

nodes.label: Property Financing
nodes.description: Financing services, case management, and sales processes with the purpose of financing properties, often identified by the property being used as collateral. Depending on market and case type, the property financing cases include products from other product domains, e.g. Realkredit Loans, Bank Loans & Credit Facilities, and Accounts.

nodes.label: Bank & Credit Facilities
nodes.description: Management of bank-based loan products and credit facility products, funded in various ways, e.g. via customer savings, covered bonds, or senior unsecured bonds. Includes product capabilities, lifecycle processes, and supporting processes for funding and financial risk handling.

nodes.label: Asset Finance
nodes.description: Financing of tangible assets such as vehicles and machinery, where the title to the asset either remains with the financier during the contract period (leasing), or where special title transfer and repossession conditions apply (hire purchase).

nodes.label: Consumer Financing
nodes.description: Financing services, case management, and sales processes specifically targeted Personal Customers for other purposes than property financing (e.g. car loans, credit cards, overdrafts, and similar consumer financing needs). Consumer financing cases include products from other product domains, primarily Bank Loans & Credit Facilities, Cards and Accounts. Financing can be securitized or non-securitized (with or without collateral).

nodes.label: Investment Products
nodes.description: Provide investment product construction, fund investment management and institutional investment services

nodes.label: Mutual Fund Administration
nodes.description: Management and oversight of investment fund products covering Net Asset Value calculations, fund accounting and authority reporting.

nodes.label: Institutional Investment Services
nodes.description: Investment services provided to institutional investment customers to analyse, plan, and establish an investment offering. The result is an agreement and services supporting that agreement that governs the subsequent investment activity.

nodes.label: Investment Management Execution
nodes.description: An ongoing sell-side trading activity to initiate trading of securities within the customer investment portfolio to take advantage of market conditions, remaining within the agreed investment policies governing the portfolio

nodes.label: Accounts
nodes.description: The basic ability to create types of accounts whether as business products or as internal tallies and manage and enforce account rules. This is core functionality for customer accounts, bookkeeping accounts, accounting, and probably other domains.

nodes.label: Fees
nodes.description: Management and application of fees

nodes.label: Accounting
nodes.description: Managing chart of accounts and registering all actions involving money according to said plan. Lowest level reconsiliation (debit plus credit must equal 0). Higher level reconciliation (purpose defined accounts balance) probably belongs here as well

nodes.label: Interest
nodes.description: Management and application of interest and associated calculation rules for accounts

nodes.label: Account Products
nodes.description: Management of account products that can served as standalone business products or act as a building block in complex business products

nodes.label: Account Infrastructure
nodes.description: Management of accounts that can be used for any business product and internal accounting

nodes.label: Cash Management
nodes.description: Collecting and managing cash flows

nodes.label: Cash Pool
nodes.description: Management of cash flows across multiple accounts to optimise cost of capital

nodes.label: Liquidity Management
nodes.description: Management of cash flows to reduce liquidity risk

nodes.label: Payments
nodes.description: Register and orchestrate the execution of payment transactions, with, and between bank using any appropriate payment mechanism

nodes.label: Payment Execution
nodes.description: Management of payment request validation, authorisation, reservations

nodes.label: Payment Initiation Management
nodes.description: Management of payment initiation requests

nodes.label: Payment Clearing & Settlement Management
nodes.description: Management of clearing and settlement and payments with correspondent banks and payment industry clearing networks and clearing houses

nodes.label: Payment Servicing
nodes.description: Payment activity monitoring, investigations, and exceptions management

nodes.label: Insurance
nodes.description: Financial compensation in the event of designated risks materialising

nodes.label: Payment Protection Insurance
nodes.description: Management of payment protection insurance products and agreements

nodes.label: Cards
nodes.description: Activity related to the issuing and administration of credit- and debit cards.

nodes.label: Card Acquiring
nodes.description:
Card Acquiring as part of Card Payment management.
Deliver service in the role of card acquirer.

nodes.label: Card Payment Processing
nodes.description: Management of clearing and settlement and authorised card transactions with card processors.

nodes.label: Card Production
nodes.description:
Card production is producing and delivering new card to card holder. Propagating the card so it's accepted by all links in the chain - pls. refer the "Four corner model".
Card delivery management, Order quote management. The agreements with physical "card embossing/printing" companies and physical card designs and chip profiles used in the manufacturing, e.g., chip-versions on different cards, monitor stock level of plastic card designs etc.

nodes.label: Card Lifecycle
nodes.description:
Establishing and everyday handling of card agreements between customers and Danske Bank.
This includes Agreement definitions, Agreement lifecycle management, Agreement terms and conditions.
Furthermore, Cardholder agreements and corporate agreements. Defines the agreement blueprints and the lifecycle of these. Owns the agreement between customers and Danske bank as card issuer.
Issuing of cards and everyday handling of card configuration. Activation, Block/Unblock, Renewal, Expire, View Pin, Wallet features etc. This is management of the card in the hands of the card holder - except the actual use for payments (see Card Authorization).
Card status management, Card application management, Card wallet management.
The Card, incl. PIN, Card-as-token.

nodes.label: Card Agreement
nodes.description: Manage card agreements with our customers and card holders concerning the cards they hold and associated value-add services.

nodes.label: Card Collection
nodes.description:
Issuing Credit card statements, invoicing and collections based on payment balance. Collection of annual card fee. Keeping special solutions for "book-now-pay-later", individual credit days for corporates.
Collection management
Expense management (for customers).
Card Collections and Statements.
Credit days for corporate customers.
Instalments (the individual customers option to chop a large payment into smaller payments)

nodes.label: Card Product
nodes.description:
Maintain card product blueprints including value added offerings around a card product.
This comprises Card product management, Card portfolio management, Card scheme management and Value-added service management.
Management of default Fees and prices, definition of terms and conditions and the agreements with card schemes.
Management of the agreements with partners who deliver value-added services.

nodes.label: Card Rewards & Loyalty
nodes.description:
Cash-back solutions in various forms and benefits via co-branding (membership benefits like SAS travel points, discount on annual fee etc.)
Customer loyalty management.
The agreements with partners on benefit programs.
Membership relations.
Cash-back (a generic word for discount steps, benefits gained, paid-out discounts etc.)

nodes.label: Banking Services
nodes.description: Services rendered to customer about their banking business activity, including deposit and withdrawal of cash and cheques, management of safe deposits, management of the customer's tax resulting for their banking activity

nodes.label: Safe Deposit Box
nodes.description: Management of safe deposit boxes

nodes.label: Cheques
nodes.description: Management, issuance, and collection of cheques

nodes.label: Customer Tax Management
nodes.description: Management and reporting of customer's tax concerning their business with the bank

nodes.label: Cash Currency Exchange
nodes.description: Management of purchase and sale of foreign currency in cash

nodes.label: Consultancy
nodes.description: Consultancy services for customers about their financial life

nodes.label: Business Statements
nodes.description: Management of business statements for customers such as audit letter and year end business statement

nodes.label: Life & Pension
nodes.description: Pension plans and life insurance covering the events of losing work ability, dying before retirement, and retirement

nodes.label: Life & Pension Products
nodes.description: Management and catalogue of life insurance and pension saving products including price calculations

nodes.label: Life & Pension Offers
nodes.description: Management of offers for life & pension agreements

nodes.label: Life & Pension Risk Accounting
nodes.description: Management of risk accounting to determine how much capital is required to support existing life & pension agreements.

nodes.label: Life & Pension Disbursements
nodes.description: Management of disbursement of life & pension agreements including tax

nodes.label: Life & Pension Collection & Payment
nodes.description: Management of collection and payment that are required to maintain a life & pension agreement

nodes.label: Life & Pension Investment Portfolio
nodes.description: Management of the investment portfolio for life & pension collective and individual agreements

nodes.label: Life & Pension Beneficiaries
nodes.description: Management of beneficiaries on life & pension agreements

nodes.label: Channels
nodes.description: Channels are the touchpoints where customers and partners can interact with the Bank's products and services

nodes.label: Human Channels
nodes.description: Support of the interactions that take place on customer-facing channels, e.g., a call-center, a chat

nodes.label: Help Desk
nodes.description: Operational overview of online, digital solutions

nodes.label: Contact Centres
nodes.description: Capabilities to provide fast and easy access for contact centre agents to customer overview and our sources of information

nodes.label: Branch Services
nodes.description: Management of branches and branch-based customer transactions

nodes.label: Teller
nodes.description: Handling of cash, payment requests, cheques at the branch counter

nodes.label: ATMs
nodes.description: An Automated Teller Machine is a customer interface that provides the ability to withdraw cash in local and foreign currency, to deposit cash, and perform basic actions for the customer's accounts such as show current balance.

nodes.label: Digital Self-Service Channels
nodes.description: Channels where the customers is self-serviced without a bank employee as supporter or counterpart

nodes.label: Web & Mobile Banking
nodes.description: Online digital solutions serving individual customers.

nodes.label: Open Banking
nodes.description: Open banking portal and APIs

nodes.label: Third Party Authentication for Open Banking
nodes.description: Authentication of access to Open Banking APIs

nodes.label: Open Banking APIs
nodes.description: Open Banking APIs - those that are made available to external parties, e.g., under the UK Open Banking and EU PSD2 regulations

nodes.label: Open Banking Developer Portal
nodes.description: Overview, documentation, and testing ground of Open Banking APIs for external parties.

nodes.label: External Information Exchange
nodes.description: Capabilities for electronic data interchange (EDI) between specific software applications of the customer and the Bank.

nodes.label: Homepages
nodes.description: Web-pages where anonymous users can access information about the Group, and its products and services. This can include interactive web-pages.

nodes.label: Communication Channels
nodes.description: Channels and contact facilities where the customer is serviced by a bank employee

nodes.label: Online Meetings
nodes.description: Meeting through electronic where meeting attendants can be at any and different physical locations.

nodes.label: Chat
nodes.description: Interactive text-based form of communication between end-user or customer and another individual or a chatbot.

nodes.label: Electronic Mail
nodes.description: Mail transmitted by electronic means.

nodes.label: SMS
nodes.description: Text messages transmitted through the SMS protocol on mobile phones

nodes.label: Physical Mail
nodes.description: Mail sent in an envelope and carried by the mail company to its receiver

nodes.label: Information Technology
nodes.description: Management, deployment, and use of computers to enable creation, processing, storage, retrieval and exchange all kinds of electronic data and information

nodes.label: IT Platform
nodes.description: The IT platform provides the foundation for application delivery

nodes.label: Solution Design & Delivery
nodes.description: Design and delivery of solutions from requirements analysis through development and engineering towards production deployment

nodes.label: Software Integration & Deployment
nodes.description: Tool chains for combining, assembling and integrating designed and constructed solutions towards deployable instances

nodes.label: Software Engineering Platforms
nodes.description: Application platforms that provide means for software engineering

nodes.label: Quality Assurance
nodes.description: Assurance and verification of the quality of IT solutions with respect to functional and non-functional requirements

nodes.label: Solution Architecture
nodes.description: Requirements analysis and design of solutions

nodes.label: Information Security
nodes.description: Prevent, detect, respond to cyberthreats

nodes.label: Security Governance
nodes.description: Security policies and their enforcement, e.g., identity and access management, physical security

nodes.label: Application Security
nodes.description: The application security measures, functionality, and the supporting processes and activities designed to mitigate and prevent cyber security threats

nodes.label: Data Security
nodes.description: Core data security protective services, e.g. key management, cryptography, certificates

nodes.label: Physical Security
nodes.description: The security measures to protect personnel, hardware, software, networks, and assets against unauthorised physical access.

nodes.label: Security Operations
nodes.description: The processes needed to manage security in an operational context

nodes.label: Endpoint Security
nodes.description: Hardware devices and software providing security services for the protection of endpoints.

nodes.label: Platform Security
nodes.description: Hardware devices and software providing security services for information in transit between endpoints

nodes.label: Identity & Access Management
nodes.description: The security measures to digitally identify and authenticate users and to control authorisations to access resources

nodes.label: IT Infrastructure
nodes.description: Provide IT infrastructure and middleware to run the bank's estate of IT applications

nodes.label: Integration and Messaging
nodes.description: Infrastructure for transmitting information and actions between solutions

nodes.label: Network
nodes.description: Hardware devices providing networking and communication services.

nodes.label: Database & Storage
nodes.description: Rename:"Database & Storage". New description

nodes.label: Workplace Technology
nodes.description: Hardware, operating systems, and basic software of devices operated directly by end-users.

nodes.label: Hosting Platforms
nodes.description: Infrastructure platforms that host computational and processing workloads

nodes.label: Data Center
nodes.description: All data center (or data room) related physical infrastructure needed to locate, operate, and connect IT hardware.

nodes.label: IT Operations
nodes.description: Operate the bank's estate of IT applications

nodes.label: IT Service Management
nodes.description: Tools and services enabling IT operations capabilities such as change management, incident management, problem management, asset management.

nodes.label: Enterprise Monitoring
nodes.description: Tools and services providing capabilities to monitor the rest of technical infrastructure.

nodes.label: IT Operations Management
nodes.description: Tools and services providing the ability to manage (deploy, run, change, decommission) technical infrastructure.

nodes.label: Analytics & Dashboards
nodes.description: Tools and services providing operational analytics, metrics, and visualization capabilities.

nodes.label: Data Platform
nodes.description: The data platform provides the foundation for managing data and gaining insights from data.

nodes.label: Analytics & AI
nodes.description: Analytics, mathematical modelling, decision trees, machine learning, model development

nodes.label: Data Lake Management
nodes.description: Repository of data stored in its original format including raw data copies of business application data and transformed data kept in the heterogeneous data store and in the data warehouse. It can hold data for any use, whether for data warehouse of normalised data or machine learning on raw data.

nodes.label: Data Warehousing
nodes.description: Data repository for reporting, analysis and decisions that integrates data from different sources based on a single, unified data model

nodes.label: Data Management
nodes.description: Management, cataloguing, and governance of data

view-ddi1q6.name: Level 1
view-ddi1q6.description: Roots and direct children only; hides level 2 and deeper.

view-6vxd6i.name: Level 2
view-6vxd6i.description: Roots through grandchildren; hides level 3 and deeper.

view-npa2zh.name: Level 3
view-npa2zh.description: Roots through level 3; hides deeper detail.
